summary: Quercus dentata, also called Japanese emperor oak, daimyo oak, or Korean oak ( Japanese: 柏, kashiwa; traditional Chinese: 柞櫟; simplified Chinese: 柞栎; pinyin: zuòlì; Korean: 떡갈나무, tteokgalnamu ), is a species of oak native to East Asia ( Japan, Korea and China ). The name of the tree is often translated as "sweet oak" in English to distinguish it from Western varieties. It is placed in section Quercus.
